title: Debate Panel Signal Flow
brief: Build a panel discussion system. Get the moderator, panelists, and audience Q&A mic into the console. Feed the PA, create a press/recording feed, and send a separate monitor feed to the moderator wedge. Avoid confusing RF antenna jacks with audio outputs.
processorLabel: DEBATE PANEL PA / PRESS / MODERATOR MONITOR

educationalFeedback.wireless-receiver-antenna-a->console-inp: This is RF, not audio. Use the receiver's audio output.
educationalFeedback.wireless-receiver-antenna-b->console-inp: This is RF, not audio. Use the receiver's audio output.
educationalFeedback.wireless-receiver-ch1-audio-out->left-sp: Receiver output is line-level and needs the console/mix path before the PA.
educationalFeedback.wireless-receiver-ch2-audio-out->pa-proc: Receiver output is line-level and needs the console/mix path before the PA.
educationalFeedback.pa-processor-amp-l-output->press-recorde: This is speaker-level output and can overload or damage recording inputs.
educationalFeedback.pa-processor-amp-r-output->press-recorde: This is speaker-level output and can overload or damage recording inputs.
educationalFeedback.console-main-l-output->moderator-wedge-i: The wedge should receive a monitor/aux mix, not the main audience mix.
educationalFeedback.console-main-r-output->moderator-wedge-i: The wedge should receive a monitor/aux mix, not the main audience mix.
educationalFeedback.console-aux-1-output->pa-processor-amp-l: Aux sends are monitor mixes here. The PA processor should receive the main L/R mix.
educationalFeedback.press-recorder-output->console-input-4: This reverses the signal direction. The recorder should receive a feed from the console.
educationalFeedback.left-speaker-thru->console-input-1: Speaker outputs are not console inputs. Follow signal from source to mix to amplifier to speaker.

puzzle.scenario: You are wiring a four-person debate panel with wireless lavs, an audience Q&A handheld, a room PA, a moderator wedge, and a press recorder feed.
puzzle.objective: Route each wireless receiver audio output into the console, feed the room PA through the processor/amp path, send a clean stereo press feed, and provide a separate moderator monitor feed.

constraints.text: Wireless receiver antenna jacks carry RF, not console-ready audio.
The press recorder needs a line-level matrix/record feed, not a speaker-level amplifier output.
The moderator wedge should receive a monitor aux feed instead of the main audience mix.
The PA processor/amp path should receive the console main L/R mix before driving the speakers.

educationalFeedback.defaultWrongRoute: Trace the signal type and direction first: receiver audio to console input, console bus output to downstream input, processor output to speaker input.

routePairs.pa-processor-amp-r-output->press-recorde: This is speaker-level output and can overload or damage recording inputs.
routePairs.console-main-l-output->moderator-wedge-i: The wedge should receive a monitor/aux mix, not the main audience mix.
routePairs.console-main-r-output->moderator-wedge-i: The wedge should receive a monitor/aux mix, not the main audience mix.
routePairs.console-aux-1-output->pa-processor-amp-l: Aux sends are monitor mixes here. The PA processor should receive the main L/R mix.
routePairs.press-recorder-output->console-input-4: This reverses the signal direction. The recorder should receive a feed from the console.
routePairs.left-speaker-thru->console-input-1: Speaker outputs are not console inputs. Follow signal from source to mix to amplifier to speaker.

concepts.rf-vs-audio: RF antenna jacks are not balanced audio outputs. Use the receiver's audio output after the wireless receiver converts RF to audio.
concepts.wrong-signal-type: Match the signal level and type before patching: mic/line, bus output, processor input, amplifier output, or speaker input.
concepts.wrong-bus: Choose the console bus that matches the destination. Main outputs feed the audience PA, auxes feed monitor mixes, and matrix/record outputs feed press or recorder paths.
concepts.wrong-direction: Follow the signal direction from source to console to destination. Outputs feed inputs.
concepts.speaker-level-unsafe: Speaker-level outputs belong at speakers and can overload line-level recorder or console inputs.
concepts.processor-chain: The room PA path runs from console main outputs into the processor/amp inputs, then from amp outputs to speaker inputs.

endpointTypes.rf-output->line-input: RF outputs are not line-level audio inputs. Patch from the receiver audio output instead.
endpointTypes.speaker-output->line-input: Speaker-level output should not feed a line-level recorder or console input.
endpointTypes.main-output->monitor-input: A monitor wedge should receive an aux/monitor output, not the main audience mix.

puzzle.completionExplanation: The receiver audio outputs feed console inputs, the console main L/R outputs feed the PA processor/amp, the amp outputs drive the speakers, the matrix/record outputs feed the press recorder, and Aux 1 feeds the moderator wedge. RF antenna jacks, speaker-level outputs, and reversed recorder paths are intentionally avoided.

labels.text: Debate Panel Signal Flow: route wireless audio through the console before distributing PA, press, and moderator monitor feeds.
RF antenna jacks are traps, not audio outputs.
